heroHeadline: Pakistan Strikes Sayed Jamaluddin Afghani University in Asadabad — 4–7 Killed, 70–85 Wounded; Urumqi Framework Under Severe Strain
heroSubtitle: Pakistani mortar and missile strikes hit Sayed Jamaluddin Afghani University in Asadabad, Kunar province on April 28, killing 4–7 people and wounding 70–85, including approximately 30 students and professors. Pakistan categorically denied the strikes as 'a blatant lie.' Taliban Deputy Spokesperson Fitrat called the attack 'unforgivable war crimes against civilians and academic institutions.' Simultaneously, Pakistani shelling in Spin Boldak killed 5 more people. OCHA Situation Update #4 documented 160,000 Afghans facing heightened food insecurity and 19 health facilities closed due to the conflict. On April 29, some 1,000 trucks queued at Torkham as Pakistan's mass deportation of Afghan nationals continued at 4,000–6,000 per day, with 146,000+ expelled in 2026. Urumqi Round 2 discussions are on hold pending diplomatic clarification of the April 28 incidents.
footerNote: Data sourced from UNAMA, SIGAR, ACLED, Brown University Costs of War Project, US DoD, Reuters, BBC, Tolo News, Dawn, Al Jazeera, HRW, OHCHR, OCHA
